message:
feat(analytics): add command categorization for task-type analytics

Adds categorizeCommand() function that classifies agent commands into
meaningful task types for performance analytics:

- git: All git operations (push, pull, commit, etc.)
- build_test: npm/bun commands, test runs, builds, compilation
- search: grep, ripgrep (rg), find, glob operations
- read: File read operations (cat, head, Read tool)
- write: File write operations (sed, patch, Edit tool)
- execute: Shell execution (bash, sh, exec)
- other: Unclassified commands

Pattern matching uses order-of-specificity to avoid false positives
(e.g., "git push" checked before shell patterns that might match "sh").

Also improves percentile rank calculation:
- Removes unnecessary sort operation (O(n) instead of O(n log n))
- Clarifies semantics: 90th percentile means outperforming 90% of fleet

This enables task-type breakdown in agent performance dashboards,
helping identify which types of operations correlate with productivity.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
